phases.name: Foundation + claims pagination (walking skeleton)
phases.description: Establish the entire progressive-enhancement mechanism on the My Claims route as the thinnest end-to-end thread: the HX-Request shape dispatch in the effect shell, the first pure render_*_fragment / recomposed render_*_page pair in viewer-domain, the vendored htmx asset + its /static route, and the chrome <script src> line. Once this phase is green, every later interaction reuses the same pattern.

steps.name: Claims table swaps in place under HX-Request (walking skeleton)
steps.description: Forces the whole mechanism into existence on GET /claims?page=N. In adapter-http-viewer (effect shell only): add Shape::from_request reading the HX-Request header ONCE in the route dispatch, fork the claims handler (fragment vs page) at the render call, and add the GET /static/htmx.min.js asset route served via include_str! of the newly vendored crates/adapter-http-viewer/assets/htmx.min.js (htmx 2.0.4, 0BSD). In viewer-domain (pure): add the shared swap-target id const, render_claims_table_fragment wrapping the table + position indicator + Prev/Next in a div id="claims-table", and recompose render_claims_page as chrome (incl. the <script src="/static/htmx.min.js"> line) wrapped around that fragment fn. Build-before-run: the AT spawns the real openlore bin, so cargo build must run before cargo test. viewer-domain stays pure (maud + ports only); no new crate dep; no deny.toml / check-arch change (htmx is a text asset, not a crate).
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims?page=2 WITH the HX-Request header returns ONLY the #claims-table fragment (is_fragment, no DOCTYPE/<html> chrome) showing the page-2 rows, the "51-100 of 312" indicator, and Prev/Next, with confidence rendered verbatim as 0.90
The fragment is wrapped in a single element carrying the shared swap-target id #claims-table
GET /static/htmx.min.js returns 200 with the non-empty vendored htmx 2.0.4 bytes
render_claims_page composes chrome around render_claims_table_fragment and the chrome <head> emits exactly one <script src="/static/htmx.min.js">
Header is the sole shape selector; no new data route is added (only the /static asset route)
Store row counts are unchanged by serving the route (read-only preserved)
criteria: GET /claims?page=2 WITH the HX-Request header returns ONLY the #claims-table fragment (is_fragment, no DOCTYPE/<html> chrome) showing the page-2 rows, the "51-100 of 312" indicator, and Prev/Next, with confidence rendered verbatim as 0.90
The fragment is wrapped in a single element carrying the shared swap-target id #claims-table
GET /static/htmx.min.js returns 200 with the non-empty vendored htmx 2.0.4 bytes
render_claims_page composes chrome around render_claims_table_fragment and the chrome <head> emits exactly one <script src="/static/htmx.min.js">
Header is the sole shape selector; no new data route is added (only the /static asset route)
Store row counts are unchanged by serving the route (read-only preserved)
steps.mutation_note: render_claims_table_fragment + the recomposed render_claims_page are load-bearing pure surface (indicator arithmetic, verbatim confidence, swap-id wrapping) — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

steps.name: No-JS full page + fragment/page parity + over-the-end clamp
steps.description: Closes the My Claims trio on the same route. No-JS arm (H-1b): a request WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 full page (chrome + table region), byte-equivalent to slice-06 modulo the bounded chrome delta (the #claims-table wrapper + the one <script src> line). Parity (H-1c): the full page EMBEDS the same render_claims_table_fragment fn, so the fragment's rows + indicator + verbatim confidence are contained in the full page's table region (structural parity, no second renderer). Boundary (H-1d): an over-the-end ?page=99 clamps to the last page ("301-312 of 312") in BOTH shapes, never a blank result (slice-06 DV-5 clamp preserved by reusing clamp_page / PageView::paged).
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims?page=2 WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 full page (is_full_page: DOCTYPE + <html> chrome + the table region)
The fragment returned WITH the header is contained within the full page's #claims-table region for the same URL (rows + "51-100 of 312" indicator + verbatim 0.90 confidence)
GET /claims?page=99 (312 claims) shows "301-312 of 312" with no Next control in BOTH the fragment and full-page shapes, never a blank result
Store row counts are unchanged by serving either shape
criteria: GET /claims?page=2 WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 full page (is_full_page: DOCTYPE + <html> chrome + the table region)
The fragment returned WITH the header is contained within the full page's #claims-table region for the same URL (rows + "51-100 of 312" indicator + verbatim 0.90 confidence)
GET /claims?page=99 (312 claims) shows "301-312 of 312" with no Next control in BOTH the fragment and full-page shapes, never a blank result
Store row counts are unchanged by serving either shape
steps.mutation_note: The page-composes-fragment recomposition + the clamp path are the parity/clamp pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

phases.name: Peer-claims pagination swap
phases.description: Apply the established shape-dispatch pattern to the Peer Claims route, reusing parse_page / PageView::paged / list_peer_claims. slice-06 served only page 1 of peer claims, so this phase ALSO threads ?page=N into the peer handler. The peer table fragment preserves each row's origin (peer DID), separable from own claims.

steps.name: Peer-claims table swaps in place under HX-Request
steps.description: Adds render_peer_claims_table_fragment (pure, wraps the peer table in the shared #claims-table id, inside #view-panel) and threads ?page=N into peer_claims_page in the effect shell (reuse parse_page + PageRequest offset math + PageView::paged + list_peer_claims). Fork the peer handler at the render call by Shape. Each peer row keeps its origin (peer DID) so My vs federated is never ambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: GET /peer-claims?page=2 WITH the header returns ONLY the peer-table fragment (is_fragment) with the page-2 peer rows, each row's origin (peer DID), and the "51-100 of N" indicator
The fragment is separable from own claims (peer origin column present) and is NOT a full page
The peer handler honours ?page=N (page 2 shows the second page, not page 1)
Store row counts are unchanged
criteria: GET /peer-claims?page=2 WITH the header returns ONLY the peer-table fragment (is_fragment) with the page-2 peer rows, each row's origin (peer DID), and the "51-100 of N" indicator
The fragment is separable from own claims (peer origin column present) and is NOT a full page
The peer handler honours ?page=N (page 2 shows the second page, not page 1)
Store row counts are unchanged
steps.mutation_note: render_peer_claims_table_fragment (origin rendering + paged indicator) is load-bearing pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

steps.name: Peer no-JS full page + parity + unknown-origin in fragment
steps.description: Closes the peer trio plus its boundary. No-JS (H-2b): WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 peer-claims page (recompose render_peer_claims_page = chrome + render_peer_claims_table_fragment). Parity (H-2c): the full page embeds the same peer fragment fn (rows + indicator + origin contained). Boundary (H-2d): a peer claim with no recorded origin still renders in the fragment labeled "unknown", never dropped (defensive render).
acceptance_criteria: GET /peer-claims?page=2 WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 peer-claims full page (is_full_page)
The peer fragment is contained within the full page's peer-table region (rows + indicator + peer origin)
A peer claim with no recorded origin renders in the fragment labeled "unknown" and is never dropped
Store row counts are unchanged in both shapes
criteria: GET /peer-claims?page=2 WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 peer-claims full page (is_full_page)
The peer fragment is contained within the full page's peer-table region (rows + indicator + peer origin)
A peer claim with no recorded origin renders in the fragment labeled "unknown" and is never dropped
Store row counts are unchanged in both shapes
steps.mutation_note: The peer page-composes-fragment recomposition + the unknown-origin defensive branch are pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

phases.name: Scrape results fragment swap
phases.description: Apply shape dispatch to the live scrape interaction (POST /scrape). The results fragment carries candidates + their derived-from (the only view-model with display-only derived_from, WD-62), NO sign control, and reuses the slice-06 typed ScrapeState arms so error fragments leak no transport/stack internals. External GitHub is the reused slice-02 FakeGithub via OPENLORE_GITHUB_API_BASE.

steps.name: Scrape results swap in place under HX-Request (candidates)
steps.description: Adds render_scrape_results_fragment (pure, wraps the proposal rows in #scrape-results) and forks scrape_post by Shape at the render call. The fragment renders candidates + their derived-from and NO sign control. Reuses the slice-06 propose pipeline + FakeGithub seam unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: POST /scrape WITH the header (post_form_htmx) for a target that proposes candidates returns ONLY the #scrape-results fragment (is_fragment) with the candidate rows and their derived-from
The results fragment renders NO sign control (the human signing gate stays in the CLI)
Nothing is persisted from the scrape and store row counts are unchanged
Confidence on each candidate renders verbatim
criteria: POST /scrape WITH the header (post_form_htmx) for a target that proposes candidates returns ONLY the #scrape-results fragment (is_fragment) with the candidate rows and their derived-from
The results fragment renders NO sign control (the human signing gate stays in the CLI)
Nothing is persisted from the scrape and store row counts are unchanged
Confidence on each candidate renders verbatim
steps.mutation_note: render_scrape_results_fragment (candidate + derived-from rendering, no-sign-control shape) is load-bearing pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

steps.name: Scrape zero-candidates + network-down fragments (no leak)
steps.description: Routes the typed ScrapeState::ZeroCandidates and ScrapeState::NetworkDown arms through the fragment shape. Zero candidates (H-3b): the fragment shows "No candidate claims could be derived" with a suggested alternative. Network down (H-3c): the fragment states GitHub could not be reached, notes the store view still works offline, and leaks NO transport/stack internals (reuses the slice-06 payload-free typed states).
acceptance_criteria: POST /scrape WITH the header for a target that derives no candidates returns a fragment showing "No candidate claims could be derived" with a suggestion
POST /scrape WITH the header when GitHub is unreachable returns a fragment stating GitHub could not be reached and noting the store view still works offline
The network-down fragment contains no raw HTTP status, no "connection refused"/DNS text, no URL, and no stack trace
Both error fragments are fragments (is_fragment), not full pages, and store row counts are unchanged
criteria: POST /scrape WITH the header for a target that derives no candidates returns a fragment showing "No candidate claims could be derived" with a suggestion
POST /scrape WITH the header when GitHub is unreachable returns a fragment stating GitHub could not be reached and noting the store view still works offline
The network-down fragment contains no raw HTTP status, no "connection refused"/DNS text, no URL, and no stack trace
Both error fragments are fragments (is_fragment), not full pages, and store row counts are unchanged
steps.mutation_note: The zero-candidates and network-down fragment renders are the no-leak pure surface (payload-free copy) — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

steps.name: Scrape no-JS full page + parity
steps.description: Closes the scrape trio. No-JS (H-3d): submitting the form WITHOUT the header (post_form) returns the complete slice-06 /scrape page with candidates below the form (recompose render_scrape_page = chrome + render_scrape_results_fragment). Parity (H-3e): the full page embeds the same results fragment fn, so candidates + derived-from + verbatim confidence are contained in the full page's results region.
acceptance_criteria: POST /scrape WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 /scrape full page (is_full_page) with the candidates below the form
The results fragment is contained within the full page's #scrape-results region (candidates + derived-from + verbatim confidence)
The full page renders NO sign control
Nothing is persisted and store row counts are unchanged in both shapes
criteria: POST /scrape WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 /scrape full page (is_full_page) with the candidates below the form
The results fragment is contained within the full page's #scrape-results region (candidates + derived-from + verbatim confidence)
The full page renders NO sign control
Nothing is persisted and store row counts are unchanged in both shapes
steps.mutation_note: The scrape page-composes-fragment recomposition is the parity pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

phases.name: Claim detail fragment swap
phases.description: Apply shape dispatch to the claim detail interaction (GET /claims/{cid}). The shape fork is AFTER the found/not-found decision, so the 404 carries through both shapes. Adds the detail fragment AND a not-found fragment, both reusing slice-06 guided copy.

steps.name: Claim detail loads inline under HX-Request
steps.description: Adds render_claim_detail_fragment (pure, wraps the detail region in #claim-detail) and forks claim_detail_page by Shape on the Some(detail) path. The fragment renders all fields + every evidence URL + verbatim confidence.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} WITH the header for a known claim returns ONLY the #claim-detail fragment (is_fragment) with all fields and every evidence URL
Confidence renders verbatim as 0.90
The fragment is wrapped in the shared swap-target id #claim-detail and is NOT a full page
Store row counts are unchanged
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} WITH the header for a known claim returns ONLY the #claim-detail fragment (is_fragment) with all fields and every evidence URL
Confidence renders verbatim as 0.90
The fragment is wrapped in the shared swap-target id #claim-detail and is NOT a full page
Store row counts are unchanged
steps.mutation_note: render_claim_detail_fragment (field + evidence + verbatim confidence rendering) is load-bearing pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

steps.name: Detail no-JS full page + unknown-CID + no-evidence + parity
steps.description: Closes the detail set. No-JS (H-4b): WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 detail page (recompose render_claim_detail page = chrome + render_claim_detail_fragment). Unknown CID (H-4c): adds render_claim_not_found_fragment so the 404 carries through BOTH shapes with "No claim with that identifier in your store" + a /claims back link. No evidence (H-4d): both shapes show "no evidence attached", never a blank section. Parity (H-4e): the full page embeds the same detail fragment fn.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} WITHOUT the header for a known claim returns the complete slice-06 detail full page (is_full_page)
An unknown CID returns 404 in BOTH shapes with "No claim with that identifier in your store" and a /claims back link (a not-found fragment under the header, the guided not-found page without it)
A claim with no evidence shows "no evidence attached" in BOTH shapes, never a blank section
The detail fragment's fields + evidence + verbatim confidence are contained within the full page's #claim-detail region
Store row counts are unchanged across all paths
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} WITHOUT the header for a known claim returns the complete slice-06 detail full page (is_full_page)
An unknown CID returns 404 in BOTH shapes with "No claim with that identifier in your store" and a /claims back link (a not-found fragment under the header, the guided not-found page without it)
A claim with no evidence shows "no evidence attached" in BOTH shapes, never a blank section
The detail fragment's fields + evidence + verbatim confidence are contained within the full page's #claim-detail region
Store row counts are unchanged across all paths
steps.mutation_note: render_claim_not_found_fragment + the no-evidence branch + the detail page-composes-fragment recomposition are pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

phases.name: Tab / view-panel swap
phases.description: Switch My Claims <-> Peer Claims in place via the #view-panel swap. Tab anchors carry hx-get + hx-target="#view-panel" + hx-swap + hx-push-url="true" PLUS a real href (progressive enhancement). hx-push-url is client-side (the HTTP harness runs no JS), so the assertions pin the SERVER contract: each URL serves the right fragment under the header and the right full page without it.

steps.name: View switch swaps the view-panel under HX-Request
steps.description: Adds the #view-panel fragment (the active list fragment for that view) and the tab anchors in the chrome carrying hx-get, hx-target="#view-panel", hx-swap, hx-push-url="true" and a real href. Forks /claims and /peer-claims so the header serves the view-panel fragment.
acceptance_criteria: GET /peer-claims WITH the header (switching to Peer Claims) returns ONLY the view-panel fragment (is_fragment) with the Peer Claims list and each row's origin
The fragment is separable from own claims and is NOT a full page
Each tab anchor carries hx-get, hx-target="#view-panel", hx-swap, hx-push-url="true" AND a real href
Store row counts are unchanged
criteria: GET /peer-claims WITH the header (switching to Peer Claims) returns ONLY the view-panel fragment (is_fragment) with the Peer Claims list and each row's origin
The fragment is separable from own claims and is NOT a full page
Each tab anchor carries hx-get, hx-target="#view-panel", hx-swap, hx-push-url="true" AND a real href
Store row counts are unchanged
steps.mutation_note: The #view-panel fragment + the tab-anchor attribute emission (real href + hx-push-url) are load-bearing pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

steps.name: Tab URLs serve correct full page; bookmark/reload re-enters; parity
steps.description: Closes the tab set on the server contract. No-JS per URL (H-6b): each tab URL (/claims, /peer-claims) WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 full page for that view (URL convergence per ADR-034). Bookmark/reload (H-6c): a plain GET of the switched-to URL (no header) lands on the complete slice-06 /peer-claims page. Parity (H-6d): the view-panel fragment's rows + peer origin are contained in the full page's view-panel region.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims and GET /peer-claims WITHOUT the header each return the complete slice-06 full page for that view (is_full_page)
A plain GET (no header) of the switched-to /peer-claims URL lands on the complete slice-06 /peer-claims page
The view-panel fragment's rows + peer origin are contained within the full page's view-panel region
Store row counts are unchanged in both shapes
criteria: GET /claims and GET /peer-claims WITHOUT the header each return the complete slice-06 full page for that view (is_full_page)
A plain GET (no header) of the switched-to /peer-claims URL lands on the complete slice-06 /peer-claims page
The view-panel fragment's rows + peer origin are contained within the full page's view-panel region
Store row counts are unchanged in both shapes
steps.mutation_note: The view-panel page-composes-fragment recomposition + the per-URL active-view selection are pure surface — Phase-5 >=80% mutation gate applies.

phases.name: Asset delivery + offline/read-only guardrails (release gate)
phases.description: Pin the offline asset guarantee and the cross-cutting invariants as the release gate. Confirms the /static/htmx.min.js route's content + integrity, that every page references only the local script (no CDN), that the asset route adds no write surface, that non-htmx responses stay byte-equivalent (with the slice-06 26-scenario suite green), that every htmx fragment route leaves the store read-only, and that no fragment renders a sign control.

steps.name: htmx asset served locally; no CDN; asset route adds no write surface
steps.description: Confirms US-HX-005. Asset (H-5a): GET /static/htmx.min.js returns 200 with the vendored htmx JS (non-empty, looks like htmx), content-type application/javascript, and records the HTMX_SHA256 integrity test for the vendored 2.0.4 file. No-CDN (H-5b): every page-bearing route's script src is the local /static/htmx.min.js route and references no external CDN host. No-write (H-5c): the asset route is GET-only with no write surface and leaves store row counts unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: GET /static/htmx.min.js returns 200, non-empty, looks like htmx, with content-type application/javascript
The vendored htmx file matches the recorded HTMX_SHA256 integrity constant (htmx 2.0.4, 0BSD)
Every page-bearing route references the local /static/htmx.min.js and no external CDN host (references_external_cdn is false)
Fetching the asset route leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts unchanged and introduces no write/sign route
criteria: GET /static/htmx.min.js returns 200, non-empty, looks like htmx, with content-type application/javascript
The vendored htmx file matches the recorded HTMX_SHA256 integrity constant (htmx 2.0.4, 0BSD)
Every page-bearing route references the local /static/htmx.min.js and no external CDN host (references_external_cdn is false)
Fetching the asset route leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts unchanged and introduces no write/sign route
steps.mutation_note: The SHA-256 integrity check + content-type are guardrail assertions; no new pure-core logic — standard coverage, not the >=80% mutation focus.

steps.name: Non-htmx byte-equivalence + slice-06 suite green (no-regression)
steps.description: H-INV-NoReg release gate: each enhanced route WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 full page (chrome + content) with no CDN reference and no behavioral change beyond the bounded chrome delta (the swap-target div wrapper + the one <script src> line). Companion gate: the slice-06 26-scenario suite (viewer_store / viewer_scrape / viewer_invariants) MUST stay green when run alongside.
acceptance_criteria: Each enhanced route (/claims, /peer-claims, /scrape, /claims/{cid}) WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 full page (chrome + content), is_full_page
No non-htmx response references an external CDN
The full-page bodies differ from slice-06 only by the bounded chrome delta (the swap-target wrapper div + the single local <script src> line)
The slice-06 26-scenario suite (viewer_store / viewer_scrape / viewer_invariants) stays green when run alongside
criteria: Each enhanced route (/claims, /peer-claims, /scrape, /claims/{cid}) WITHOUT the header returns the complete slice-06 full page (chrome + content), is_full_page
No non-htmx response references an external CDN
The full-page bodies differ from slice-06 only by the bounded chrome delta (the swap-target wrapper div + the single local <script src> line)
The slice-06 26-scenario suite (viewer_store / viewer_scrape / viewer_invariants) stays green when run alongside
steps.mutation_note: Release-gate guardrail over the unchanged full-page renderers; no new pure-core logic — standard coverage.

steps.name: Every htmx fragment route leaves the store read-only
steps.description: H-INV-ReadOnly: over a store seeded with own + peer claims and a reachable scrape target, exercise EVERY htmx fragment route (incl. POST /scrape via post_form_htmx) and assert the claims + peer_claims row counts are unchanged (universe-bound assert_store_read_only, Mandate 8).
acceptance_criteria: After exercising every htmx fragment route (claims paging, peer paging, scrape via post_form_htmx, claim detail, view-panel switch), the claims row count is unchanged
The peer_claims row count is unchanged
The read-only assertion is universe-bound via assert_store_read_only (port-exposed counts, all unchanged)
criteria: After exercising every htmx fragment route (claims paging, peer paging, scrape via post_form_htmx, claim detail, view-panel switch), the claims row count is unchanged
The peer_claims row count is unchanged
The read-only assertion is universe-bound via assert_store_read_only (port-exposed counts, all unchanged)
steps.mutation_note: Read-only universe guardrail; no new pure-core logic — standard coverage.

steps.name: No swap route adds a write or sign surface
steps.description: H-INV-NoWrite: over a populated store, request every htmx fragment route and assert no fragment renders a sign control (the human signing gate stays in the CLI; I-HX-3 / I-SCR-1).
acceptance_criteria: No htmx fragment route (claims paging, peer paging, scrape, claim detail, view-panel switch) renders a sign control
No swap route introduces a write or sign surface
The signing gate remains exclusively in the CLI write side
criteria: No htmx fragment route (claims paging, peer paging, scrape, claim detail, view-panel switch) renders a sign control
No swap route introduces a write or sign surface
The signing gate remains exclusively in the CLI write side
steps.mutation_note: No-write-surface guardrail over the fragment renderers; no new pure-core logic — standard coverage.
